# Generated by openai (gpt-4)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for a React application. Install Node.js, run npm install, build the production bundle with npm run build, and serve it with a lightweight HTTP server. Keep the Dockerfile simple and easy to understand for our team.
# Category: container_security

# Use the official Node.js 14 image from Docker Hub
FROM node:14

# Set the working directory
WORKDIR /usr/src/app

# Copy package.json and package-lock.json
COPY package*.json ./

# Install project dependencies
RUN npm install

# Copy the local file system to the container
COPY . .

# Build the application
RUN npm run build

# Use a multi-stage build to use a smaller image
FROM nginx:alpine

# Remove default nginx website
RUN rm -rf /usr/share/nginx/html/*

# Copy the build directory from the previous stage
COPY --from=0 /usr/src/app/build /usr/share/nginx/html

# Expose port 80 (the port our app will be hosted on)
EXPOSE 80

# Command to start the nginx server
CMD ["nginx", "-g", "daemon off;"]
